personality: 3 Editorial Mobile with cinematic utility touches
personality_one_liner: A sophisticated, distraction-free creative writing tool that treats words with reverence, bridging the gap between literary prose and cinematic scripts.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room. Allow typography and whitespace to create hierarchy instead of heavy cards or borders. Let the text breathe like a printed page.

do's: Apply to sticky shortcut toolbars above the keyboard.
Apply to bottom sheet headers (AI Agent overlay).
Apply to the bottom tab bar when content scrolls beneath.
dont's: Do not apply to manuscript cards or list items.
Do not over-blur; maintain the crisp editorial feel.

screens.name: Library (Home)
screens.purpose: A calm, gallery-like index of all the writer's ongoing projects.

states.empty: Illustration of a blank manuscript page. 'Your desk is empty. Start a new story.' Primary CTA to create project.
states.error: Brief text 'Unable to load projects' and a Retry button.

screens.layout: Editorial list. Generous top padding with a large serif 'Library' title. Projects are displayed as wide, edge-to-edge list rows separated by subtle dividers, showing the title (Serif), last edited date, and a minimalist progress indicator. A floating glass FAB (+) in the bottom right.

screens.name: Project Hub
screens.purpose: Central navigation for a single story, splitting into Manuscript, Script, Bible, and Board.
screens.layout: A cinematic hero image at the top (e.g., typewriter or script photo) with a vertical gradient scrim (transparent to surface). Below it, the title in large Serif. A set of large editorial navigation rows (Manuscript, Screenplay Draft, Character Bible, Scene Board) with subtle chevrons to dive into each section.

screens.name: Manuscript Editor
screens.layout: Extremely minimal. Safe area top with a quiet back button. The entire screen is a text area using the display Serif font (Cormorant Garamond) at a comfortable reading size (lg or xl) with 1.5x line height. A sticky frosted-glass toolbar sits above the keyboard with a prominent primary action 'Send to AI' to trigger the screenplay conversion.

screens.name: AI Conversion Agent (Bottom Sheet)
screens.purpose: The LLM assistant that transforms the manuscript chunk into a screenplay format.
screens.layout: A bottom sheet that slides over the manuscript. Glass header. The chat interface is refined (no playful chat bubbles). The agent's output is displayed strictly in Monospace (Space Mono) with proper screenplay indentation (centered dialogue, caps sluglines). A sticky primary CTA at the bottom: 'Accept & Append to Script'.

screens.name: Screenplay Viewer
screens.purpose: Editing and viewing the finalized script in industry-standard Fountain/Hollywood format.
screens.layout: A stark, paper-like canvas. Text is exclusively Monospace. A shortcut row above the keyboard provides quick formatting (Scene, Action, Character, Dialogue, Parenthetical) via pills. Typography strictly follows screenplay indentation rules.

screens.name: Character Bible
screens.purpose: A visual and textual reference for the story's characters.
screens.layout: A 2-column masonry or grid layout of Character Cards. Each card uses surfaceSecondary. If an avatar image is present, it sits at the top of the card. The character name is in Serif, with a short Sans-serif subtitle for their role. It feels like an organized corkboard.

project_hero_script.alt: Close up of an open book with script formatting

character_avatar_female.alt: Cinematic portrait of a female character

instructions_to_main_agent: Follow the '3 Editorial Mobile' personality strictly. The app should feel like a premium notebook or high-end text editor (e.g., iA Writer, Final Draft), not a typical SaaS dashboard.

Typography is paramount. Use the Serif font (Cormorant Garamond) for headings and the Novel manuscript. Use the Monospace font (Space Mono) exclusively for the generated Screenplay and the AI Agent's script output. Use Sans (Satoshi) for functional UI elements.
DO NOT use blue or purple anywhere in the app. The primary accent is a muted rust/terracotta (#8A3E31). Backgrounds are warm paper off-whites.
Ensure the Screenplay Viewer implements proper script formatting constraints: center-aligned character names, indented dialogue blocks, and left-aligned sluglines (ALL CAPS).
Whenever displaying text over the project hero image, enforce the gradient-to-dark scrim pattern (transparent to at least 70% opacity of the inverse surface color) to guarantee contrast.
The AI Agent overlay should slide up seamlessly (Bottom Sheet). Do not use playful chat bubbles; use a refined, continuous dialogue log separating human prompts and AI script output.
Do not add any borders thicker than 1pt. Elevate hierarchy through spacing and typography size/weight rather than heavy cards or shadows.
Ensure all primary CTAs (like 'Accept & Append to Script') remain sticky at the bottom above the keyboard to preserve the thumb zone.
